summary.summary: Replaces the previous CoreData -> JSON string -> [CarbsEntry] pipeline with a direct CoreData -> [CarbsEntry] mapping (no JSON round-trip) [pr:nightscout/Trio#1294][commit:3fea34f0f65c][diff:149115e44762]. The change adds a new CarbsStorage method getCarbsForAlgorithm(additionalCarbs:carbsDate:) and implements mapping helpers (mapToCarbsEntry, algorithmDecimal, additionalCarbsEntry) to produce [CarbsEntry] directly from CarbEntryStored rows [file:6a2d27240b74][diff:149115e44762]. OpenAPS is updated to receive a carbsStorage dependency, to call getCarbsForAlgorithm, and to pass a [CarbsEntry] into the oref algorithm (signatures and call sites updated) [file:2ccb33745ec4][file:8e278bc2bb06][diff:149115e44762]. The old CarbEntryStored JSON encoder/Encodable extension was removed (no more encoding to JSON strings) [file:665b319e6340][diff:149115e44762]. A test suite (CarbsNativeConversionTests) was added to assert the native mapping reproduces the old JSON round-trip behavior with one deliberate, documented difference (id population) and to check millisecond date handling and decimal formatting [file:c5449df501df][diff:149115e44762]. The Xcode project was updated to include the new test file [file:a96c20924cce][diff:149115e44762].
summary.userFacingImpact: Intended: algorithm inputs should be preserved by the native mapping; the included golden tests assert field-for-field equivalence with the previous JSON round-trip except that ids are now carried from Core Data and dates are kept at full precision/millisecond resolution (these behaviors are documented and asserted in the new tests) [file:c5449df501df][commit:3fea34f0f65c][diff:149115e44762]. Conservative note: because this change alters how carb entries are constructed and delivered to the dosing algorithm (moving from a JSON string path to direct structs), there is potential for behavioral differences in downstream algorithm results; the test coverage asserts equivalence for covered cases but a human review and regression test in a staging environment is recommended before release to users [file:c5449df501df][file:6a2d27240b74][diff:149115e44762].
